name: Acorn Econet Bridge
description: The Acorn Econet Bridge is a standalone 6502-based device that joins two Econet networks, forwarding traffic between them while isolating local traffic to each side. The bridge firmware lives in an 8 KB ROM at &E000-&FFFF and implements the bridge protocols that allow file servers, printer servers, and station clients on different Econet segments to communicate.

analyses.label: Anti-aliasing in the Econet Bridge's RAM test
analyses.note: A close reading of the thirteen-instruction routine at &E00B that sizes the Bridge's RAM. The INC $00 instructions between each write and read are a layered defence against three distinct failure modes.

analyses.label: The self-test LED, driven by a repurposed ADLC pin
analyses.note: How the Bridge's single front-panel LED is driven by CR3 bit 7 on ADLC B, with exactly two writes in the whole ROM, making it a pure function of which init path was taken.

analyses.label: Escape-to-main: the Bridge's cooperative error-recovery idiom
analyses.note: Four routines share a PLA/PLA/JMP main_loop abnormal exit that drops the caller's return address and collapses any failed operation back to the main dispatcher. Trades per-site clarity for global simplicity and a meaningful ROM-space saving.

analyses.label: One frame, two broadcasts: reset-time announcement
analyses.note: The Bridge announces itself on both Econet sides at power-on by building a single frame template and transmitting it twice, patching a single byte of payload between the two transmissions. A small idiom with a clean separation of concerns between builder and transmitter.

references.label: Econet Installation Guide (0482,009 Issue 1, 27 September 1988) (PDF)
references.note: Acorn's official Econet installation guide. Chapter 3 covers the Bridge. A local copy is kept in docs/.

references.label: The Replica Acorn Econet Bridge project
references.note: A modern reproduction of the Acorn Econet Bridge, with useful photographs and build notes.

references.label: Replica Econet Bridge Schematic (PDF)
references.note: Schematic for the replica board, closely matching the original Acorn design.

references.label: Ian Stocks's reverse-engineered Acorn Econet Bridge schematic (PDF)
references.note: Schematic reverse-engineered from an original Acorn board. Local copy in docs/; originally shared in the Stardot forum thread below.

references.label: Stardot Forums: Econet Bridge schematic thread
references.note: Discussion of Ian Stocks's reverse-engineered schematic and Bridge hardware details.

references.label: Stardot Forums: discussion of the Acorn Econet Bridge ROM
references.note: Thread discussing the Bridge ROM's behaviour and internals.

references.label: Rick Murray's notes on the Acorn Econet Bridge
references.note: Overview of the Acorn Econet Bridge hardware, firmware behaviour, and configuration.

references.label: Beebmaster: The Acorn Bridge
references.note: Beebmaster's page on the Acorn Bridge, with photographs, configuration notes, and operational details.

references.label: J.G. Harston's mdfs.net Econet Bridge archive
references.note: Directory listing for the Acorn Econet Bridge on mdfs.net. As of writing, the BRIDGE.S disassembly link on the page does not resolve.

references.label: Motorola MC68B54P ADLC datasheet (PDF)
references.note: Datasheet for the Advanced Data Link Controller used on each Econet port of the Bridge. Authoritative reference for CR1-CR4 and SR1/SR2 bit meanings.
